identification: C = Brahman (nirguṇa) — sat-chit-ānanda, the non-dual ground
honest_advaita_assessment: The kernel's R₁-R₇ are nearly the Upaniṣadic mahāvākyas in mathematical form. R₁ existence ↔ sat (Chandogya 6.2.1: ekam evādvitīyam — one only, without a second). R₂ atemporality ↔ akṣara (Bṛhadāraṇyaka 3.8.8: imperishable). R₃ universality ↔ tat tvam asi (that thou art — Chandogya 6.8.7); the same Ātman = Brahman in every conscious being. R₄ sourcing ↔ yato vā imāni bhūtāni jāyante (from which these beings are born, Taittirīya 3.1). R₅ inexhaustibility ↔ pūrṇam adaḥ pūrṇam idam (that is full, this is full — Iśa invocation; subtracting fullness leaves fullness). R₆ invisibility ↔ neti neti (Bṛhadāraṇyaka 4.5.15: not this, not this — only known by what it is not). R₇ pre-input ↔ anādi (without beginning). T_bridge uniqueness ↔ advaita itself (non-duality). T_word: 'Word' in Vedānta is śabda Brahman (sound-Brahman), and the ultimate identity is between śabda Brahman and the silent param Brahman. 1 John 4:8 (God is love) ↔ ānanda (bliss), the third member of sat-chit-ānanda. The kernel walks the prasthāna-traya path (three sources: Upaniṣads, Brahma Sūtras, Gītā) in compressed mathematical form.
